b:Tc925,<div class="question">
<p>Which cell is a polyploid zygote produced by fusion of one haploid and one diploid gamete?</p>
<p style="text-align:center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/10/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","10"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"21M.1.HL.TZ2.36"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"HL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","1"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"21M"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","10-3-gene-pools-and-speciation"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\">\n<p>A</p>\n</div>"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\">\n<p>This question proved difficult and few candidates, including those scoring well on the examination, selected the correct response. The great majority of students opted for response D, presumably by thinking haploid = 1, diploid = 2 and when fused you get a cell with 3 chromosomes. For answer D to be correct the original diploid number would need to have been 2 and, in that case, the 3 chromosomes in the cell would be identical in size. Answer A is the only possible response with the original cell having 2n = 4.</p>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
